## [00:00 - 03:02]

So now we'll be talking about how to leverage your existing traffic through the X-It intent. So as a quick refresher, there's multiple ways that pop-ups are fired or flyouts or embeds are happening on your website. So a flyout, for example, a flyout can be something that corners out the very bottom, again, kind of flies out of the window. And that's something that could be triggered based off someone entering and landing on your website. That could be based off someone scrolling a specific number of seconds on your page. That could be triggered after someone scrolling maybe 50% of your website. Or alternatively, which is the one we're going to be talking about right now, is when someone's exiting. So all the tools out there have some kind of different technology around how they determine whether someone's exiting, whether the mouse is moving towards the exit sign, whatever it might be. So in this particular lecture, we're going to talk about how to leverage X-It intent to collect email subscribers and then also to drive revenue. So in a lot of ways, it's kind of like the welcome flow. It's just triggered through kind of an X-It intent instead. So I want to show you a specific example of a company called Bandit Planet and how they're driving a couple thousand dollars extra per month through the same methodology. You could see in my screen right now, they have a number of different X-It intent flows. So this top one up here, X-It intent USS, has driven $5,700 in revenue over the past three months. This one, X-It intent X-Fora, has driven $5,300. So all in all, you can see they have about a dozen of these different ones. And what they do is they trigger X-It intent upon specific product pages. So what I mean by that is now you can see on my screen here, if you're on what's called the ultimate skin spa page and you're about to exit, this window at the very, very bottom on the far right says, wait, get 70% off. So they're trying to collect the traffic and turn that traffic into an email address. And then once someone enters their email there, that will then trigger content, which I'm about to show you right now. So that email will look something like this. So hey Chase, thank you for submitting your email. Use this code and save 70% on the ultimate skin spa. We hope you enjoy this level of exfoliation, et cetera. So again, it kind of triggers like a welcome type message based off of a form, but it's a little bit different. And for that specific one I just showed you, here's the revenue driven. So over the past 30 days, it's had about a 40% open rate, a 7% click through, a 4% place to order rate, and it's at about $1,300 in revenue. So again, this company that we work with, they have about six of them, if not a dozen of these, and each one drives anywhere from a couple hundred dollars to a few thousand dollars a month. So leverage is hop up upon X-It intent to filter and send your customers messages that can help them stay around and convert. So the X-It intent form should have similar, maybe if not a little bit higher, of a discount offering to entice people to actually enter their email before they're about to bail from

## [03:02 - 04:28]

your site. And the way that I recommend, even just one simple email or two simple emails for something like this, that kind of is quick and dirty and gets to the point. So in this particular example, the subject line itself is your promotion is waiting, your promo code is waiting. So when people are opening this email, they're opening with intent to get a really great offer. Hence when they open this email about to show you right now, which I just showed you shortly, again it's very short to the point, thanks for giving us your email, here's the offer, now make a purchase. So the key here is to be very direct with your offer. And going back to the point I mentioned around potentially doing a bigger discount here, the reason I mentioned that, and I know some of you might be kind of wary of giving out again discounts at all or larger discounts than typical, but these are people that you spent money on to drive to your website that are planning on leaving. Again, there's a way in Clavio and other pop-ups to say only show this to people that are not already on my list. And that will basically prevent people on your list from getting this special offer. It will only entice new potential customers who again are on their way out. And instead of driving zero dollars in revenue, you now have a chance to drive a couple hundred dollars to potentially a couple thousand dollars a month in extra revenue. So your action item now is to go create the pop-up. And trigger it off of exit intent. You can do this within Clavio or you can do this within most of the email platforms that you can create forms within. So trigger against exit intent, create your email, set this live, and hopefully see the dollars roll in.
